# World

Venice in the early 1520s. A crowded lagoon republic ruled by the Ten, the
Senate, and the Council, with commerce moving through the Rialto and the
Arsenale running at maximum capacity. The Zecca mints ducats under guard.

Plague returns every few years. The apothecaries' guild controls the
city's medicine; the speziale at Ponte San Giovanni is said to keep a
mithridate older than the doge. The inquisitori di stato are hunting
ledger-tamperers after a fire at the mint last winter.
